summary: Created a new Ralph Site blog page targeting the high-intent query 'Claude Code autonomous mode wrapper' and linked it from the existing unattended Claude Code article, routing readers to Ralph Workflow with Codeberg first and GitHub second.

fresh_signal_used.source: Stack Overflow search / live query review
fresh_signal_used.signal: Recent high-intent demand around 'Autonomous mode / wrapper for Claude Code?' with competitor mentions in the answer stream.
fresh_signal_used.why_it_matters: This is close to evaluator pain and is more actionable right now than saturated curator/outreach lanes or another repo-surface-only tweak.

changes.change: Added a focused search-intent page explaining why an unattended Claude Code setup needs a workflow layer and positioning Ralph Workflow as the Codeberg-first answer.
Inserted an early internal link to the new exact-intent wrapper page.

why_this_action: Primary repo adoption is flat, but the repo/docs conversion surfaces were already improved earlier, so another near-duplicate repo copy pass would be lower leverage.
The strongest live demand signal available now is a practical Claude Code automation/wrapper question from evaluators close to the problem Ralph Workflow solves.
Creating a precise intent-match page gives Ralph Workflow a reusable asset for future StackOverflow, Reddit, and comparison distribution without waiting on account auth or curator response windows.
verification: Confirmed both edited files exist and start with valid markdown frontmatter.
Confirmed the existing unattended Claude Code article now links to /blog/claude-code-autonomous-mode-wrapper.
Reviewed git diff for both changed files.

expected_outcome.primary_goal: Capture and convert high-intent Claude Code autonomous-wrapper evaluators into Codeberg visits.
expected_outcome.secondary_goal: Create a linkable answer asset for future live posting when StackOverflow or other channel execution is available.
expected_outcome.measurement_hint: Watch Search Console/query logs for wrapper/autonomous/unattended Claude Code terms and any correlated Codeberg movement before producing another same-family article.
